text:
Once upon a quiet afternoon in the quaint village of Everwood, an unusual old tree known as Lady Wisdom Oak sighed deeply. This peculiar tree didn't typically lend an ear to the whimsical musings of woodland creatures. Yet today, an eager squirrel buzzed eagerly through the branches, its tiny voice pleading.

"A tale, Lady Wisdom? Surely you won't forget that?"

The squirrel's tiny voice seemed to echo through the ages. Lady Wisdom could forgive the foolishness of children, but this squirrel's persistence was quite entertaining.

"I'll conjure a story about wise old bumblebees," Lady Wisdom said thoughtfully. "One day, they flew into a forest filled with wise old owls."

The owl nearest the squirrel tilted its head in owl-wise curiosity.

"Every morning they'd sing this little ditty," the squirrel recounted. "Bees with eyes as big as lanterns swarmed their beeswax honeycombs. They'd busily hum about the virtues of humility
